page: Amazon Web Services m8id.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The CPU is equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The instance is provisioned with 32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the m8id.2xlarge includes 474 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, providing high-speed local read and write capabilities directly attached to the host. Network connectivity is configured with a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps. No dedicated GPU hardware is included in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong single-threaded performance. It achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core testing (2207.0) and single-threaded bzip3 decompression (14.58 GB/sec). Single-threaded gzip compression is also strong at 34.23 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 8583.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 8647.23. Memory bandwidth is measured at 643.45 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 71.09 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. For lightweight machine learning workloads, the instance achieves a strong 278.18 tokens/sec for text generation using a small 135M model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8id.2xlarge offers a balanced resource profile, combining dedicated Intel Xeon compute, high-speed DDR5 memory, and local NVMe SSD storage. This resource density makes it highly suitable for workloads requiring fast local scratch space and strong single-core execution. Recommended workloads include general-purpose applications, web serving, text processing, software compilation, and database operations that benefit from local instance storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m8id.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and a 3.9 GHz clock speed. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz and 474 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. In benchmarks, the instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2207.0 and exceptional single-threaded bzip3 decompression speeds. It also shows strong performance in lightweight LLM text generation. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and fast local storage, the m8id.2xlarge is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, database operations, text processing, and software compilation requiring high-speed local scratch space.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 474 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core benchmark performance.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance combining high-speed DDR5 memory, dedicated Intel Xeon processors, and fast local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 32 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory
474 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with AVX-512 and AMX support
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Ideal for general-purpose applications, databases, and software compilation
